pi_items.source: Task description explicit instructions (Aluminum = 2.70 g/cm³ = 0.0027 g/mm³).

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on 'side.slvs' from the official SolveSpace box-parts tutorial. While the task claims the file is a 2D part, it is already a 3D solid with a thickness of ~5.99 mm (volume ~155405 mm³). The base 2D area is ~25925 mm². Because the prompt requires the agent to add a new extrusion group (which the programmatic verifier strictly checks via 'new_groups > old_groups'), adding a 5.0 mm extrusion to the sketch will union with the existing 5.99 mm solid, resulting in no change to the actual volume (~155405 mm³) and mass (~419.6 g). If the agent deletes or modifies the existing extrusion to 5.0 mm, the volume would correctly become ~129625 mm³ (~350.0 g), but the verifier will erroneously fail the run for not creating a 'new' group. The VLM verifier passes any mass mathematically matching the onscreen volume multiplied by 0.0027 g/mm³, regardless of whether the volume was properly adjusted to 5.0 mm.
